prestazioni: Engine swap to a **Lancia Aurelia B20 GT-derived 2.5L V6 (Type 828.000) with a forged crankshaft (Carrillo rods, JE pistons, 11.5:1 compression) and a custom billet aluminum intake manifold (by **Tubi Style**) feeding dual **Weber 45 DCOE carburetors** with velocity stacks (K&N filters).

Full **Ohlins Road & Track DFV coilovers** with adjustable camber plates (custom-machined for the Flavia’s double-wishbone front and De Dion rear) and **Eibach Pro-Kit springs** (20% stiffer than OEM) paired with **Powerflex polyurethane bushings** (front control arms, subframe, and differential mounts).

Custom **Capristo 3-inch stainless steel exhaust system** with a **Scorpion 2.5-inch center silencer** (resonated for a deep, Euro V6 growl) and **Remus titanium rear mufflers** (dual 3.5-inch outlets), mated to a **GReddy Profec boost controller** (for future forced induction compatibility).
estetica: Full **Zagato-inspired widebody conversion** using **Liberty Walk LB Works** carbon fiber fender flares (custom-fitted to the Flavia’s 1960s proportions) with **OEM-style rivets** and a **custom-molded front splitter** (by **Novitec**) in raw carbon fiber.

Retro-modern **HRE FF01 wheels** (16x8 ET20 front, 16x9 ET25 rear) in **Gunmetal Bronze** with **Pirelli P7 Corsa Classic tires** (205/55/16 front, 225/50/16 rear) and **Brembo GT 4-piston calipers** (painted **Rosso Corsa**) clamping **330mm two-piece rotors** (with **AP Racing brake pads**).

Custom **Plexiglas headlight covers** (vintage Lancia rally style) with **Hella Rallye 1000 driving lamps** (Cibie mounting brackets) and a **carbon fiber hood** (by **Seibon**) featuring **functional NACA ducts** (feeding the Weber carbs) and a **Lancia HF quad-LED taillight conversion** (by **Dapper Lighting**).
